tossups.question: A director from one of these two countries took advantage of the other’s lax censorship to make the erotic anthology Immoral Tales. In a film set in these two countries, a green-tinged sequence depicts a teacher watching a marionette show. In another film, a seemingly-irrelevant shot of a suitcase at an airport is revealed to contain a man smuggling himself from one of these countries to the other. En route to meeting a lawyer, a film’s protagonist stumbles upon two hairdressers from these two countries divorcing due to the husband’s (*) impotence. In a film scored by a composer with the surname Preisner, a soprano and music teacher from these two countries are both played by Irène Jacob. A director from one of these two countries used a film about a composer’s grieving widow to begin a series themed on the other’s flag. For 10 points, name these two European countries that produced the Three Colours trilogy.
tossups.question_sanitized: A director from one of these two countries took advantage of the other's lax censorship to make the erotic anthology Immoral Tales. In a film set in these two countries, a green-tinged sequence depicts a teacher watching a marionette show. In another film, a seemingly-irrelevant shot of a suitcase at an airport is revealed to contain a man smuggling himself from one of these countries to the other. En route to meeting a lawyer, a film's protagonist stumbles upon two hairdressers from these two countries divorcing due to the husband's (*) impotence. In a film scored by a composer with the surname Preisner, a soprano and music teacher from these two countries are both played by Irene Jacob. A director from one of these two countries used a film about a composer's grieving widow to begin a series themed on the other's flag. For 10 points, name these two European countries that produced the Three Colours trilogy.
tossups.answer: Poland AND France [accept Polska, Republic of Poland, or Rzeczpospolita Polska in place of “Poland”; accept French Republic or République française in place of “France”] (Walerian Borowczyk directed Immortal Tales. The other films mentioned are, in order: Three Colours: White, The Double Life of Veronique, and Three Colours: Blue, all directed by Krzysztof Kieślowski and scored by Zbigniew Preisner.)
tossups.answer_sanitized: Poland AND France [accept Polska, Republic of Poland, or Rzeczpospolita Polska in place of "Poland"; accept French Republic or Republique francaise in place of "France"] (Walerian Borowczyk directed Immortal Tales. The other films mentioned are, in order: Three Colours: White, The Double Life of Veronique, and Three Colours: Blue, all directed by Krzysztof Kieslowski and scored by Zbigniew Preisner.)

tossups.question: Mark Cousins’ documentary The Story of Film begins by contrasting of the opening of Saving Private Ryan with a scene created by this director. In one film, this director’s camera shows the point of view of a woman collapsing on stage lit with green light, which cuts to a shot that flies over the heads of the audience. In another film, this director’s camera pans left from the back of the head of man taking off headphones in a store to the protagonist listening to the same song; both of those films star (*) Irene Jacob. In the 1980s, this director created a TV series about the residents of an apartment complex loosely inspired by the Ten Commandments. In a film by this director, a man from his native country seeks to get revenge on his ex-wife, played by Julie Delpy, after a court case shown in his previous film. That film is the second in a trilogy mostly set in France and based on the principles of liberty, equality, and fraternity. For 10 points, identify this Polish director of the Dekalog and Three Colours trilogy.
tossups.question_sanitized: Mark Cousins' documentary The Story of Film begins by contrasting of the opening of Saving Private Ryan with a scene created by this director. In one film, this director's camera shows the point of view of a woman collapsing on stage lit with green light, which cuts to a shot that flies over the heads of the audience. In another film, this director's camera pans left from the back of the head of man taking off headphones in a store to the protagonist listening to the same song; both of those films star (*) Irene Jacob. In the 1980s, this director created a TV series about the residents of an apartment complex loosely inspired by the Ten Commandments. In a film by this director, a man from his native country seeks to get revenge on his ex-wife, played by Julie Delpy, after a court case shown in his previous film. That film is the second in a trilogy mostly set in France and based on the principles of liberty, equality, and fraternity. For 10 points, identify this Polish director of the Dekalog and Three Colours trilogy.

tossups.question: A shot in a film directed by this man features a man playing a melancholy tune on a recorder as a woman soaks a cube of sugar in her coffee. Another of this man's films begins with a series of shots tracing the wires through which a phone call propagates before following a conversation between a model and her boyfriend. In one of this man's films, a tear rolling down a widow's eye ends a montage that is scored with the "Song for the Unification of Europe" by this man's regular collaborator Zbigniew Preisner. A series of films directed by this man ends after seven of the main characters survive a ferry accident on a shot replicating the Irene Jacob-played Valentine's chewing gum poster. The first of those films by this man opens with a car accident killing the composer Patrice de Courcy and his daughter, leaving Juliette Binoche's Julie to grieve for them. For 10 points, the miniseries The Decalogue and a trilogy consisting of the films Blue, White, and Red were directed by what Polish filmmaker?
tossups.question_sanitized: A shot in a film directed by this man features a man playing a melancholy tune on a recorder as a woman soaks a cube of sugar in her coffee. Another of this man's films begins with a series of shots tracing the wires through which a phone call propagates before following a conversation between a model and her boyfriend. In one of this man's films, a tear rolling down a widow's eye ends a montage that is scored with the "Song for the Unification of Europe" by this man's regular collaborator Zbigniew Preisner. A series of films directed by this man ends after seven of the main characters survive a ferry accident on a shot replicating the Irene Jacob-played Valentine's chewing gum poster. The first of those films by this man opens with a car accident killing the composer Patrice de Courcy and his daughter, leaving Juliette Binoche's Julie to grieve for them. For 10 points, the miniseries The Decalogue and a trilogy consisting of the films Blue, White, and Red were directed by what Polish filmmaker?

bonuses.leadin: Answer the following about doubles and doppelgangers in cinema, for 10 points each.
parts: The waitress Diane Selwyn turns out to resemble aspiring actress Betty Elms in this director's non-linear mystery Mulholland Drive. Other surrealistic, disturbing films by this man include Inland Empire and Blue Velvet.

A split screen shot of the nurse Alma and the mute actress Elisabet, suggesting the merging of their identities, concludes this Ingmar Bergman film, which opens with shots of projectors lighting up images like a crucifixion and a tarantula.

A 1991 film by this director examines the parallel lives of a Polish soprano and a French music teacher, both named Veronique. This Polish director produced the Three Colors trilogy, comprising films titled Red,White, and Blue.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Answer the following about doubles and doppelgangers in cinema, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: The waitress Diane Selwyn turns out to resemble aspiring actress Betty Elms in this director's non-linear mystery Mulholland Drive. Other surrealistic, disturbing films by this man include Inland Empire and Blue Velvet.

A split screen shot of the nurse Alma and the mute actress Elisabet, suggesting the merging of their identities, concludes this Ingmar Bergman film, which opens with shots of projectors lighting up images like a crucifixion and a tarantula.

A 1991 film by this director examines the parallel lives of a Polish soprano and a French music teacher, both named Veronique. This Polish director produced the Three Colors trilogy, comprising films titled Red,White, and Blue.

bonuses.leadin: Name some directors based on clues about their partnerships with film composers, for 10 points each.
parts: The fictional composer Van den Budenmayer was created by the composer Zbigniew Preisner and this Polish director who he frequently works with. His works include The Decalogue and the Three Colors trilogy.
John Williams has composed the music for nearly all of the films of this director of Schindler’s List and Jurassic Park.
Angelo Badalamenti was hired as Isabella Rossellini’s singing coach for this man’s movie Blue Velvet, beginning a longtime partnership. They also collaborated on Mulholland Drive.
bonuses.leadin_sanitized: Name some directors based on clues about their partnerships with film composers, for 10 points each.
parts_sanitized: The fictional composer Van den Budenmayer was created by the composer Zbigniew Preisner and this Polish director who he frequently works with. His works include The Decalogue and the Three Colors trilogy.
John Williams has composed the music for nearly all of the films of this director of Schindler's List and Jurassic Park.
Angelo Badalamenti was hired as Isabella Rossellini's singing coach for this man's movie Blue Velvet, beginning a longtime partnership. They also collaborated on Mulholland Drive.
